commonIssues:
The e-Nagar Palika / CRS portal often faces technical glitches: slow speeds, failures to save data, and OTP delivery issues.

How to fix:

  • Use e-Nagar Palika Portal or CRS Portal during early morning hours (6–9 AM).
  • Use Chrome browser, clear cache and cookies. Try a different internet connection or mobile data if OTP does not arrive.
  • Apply via MP Online Portal or visit an MP Online kiosk — they have direct backend access.
  • Helpline: 181 (MP Samadhan) | MP Online: 1800-233-1166

If death is not reported within 21 days, the process becomes complicated, requiring affidavits and First Class Magistrate/SDM approval, creating confusion.

How to fix:

  • Within 21 days: Register free at the local Nagar Nigam/Nagar Palika/Gram Panchayat office.
  • 21 days to 1 year: Submit a Notarised Affidavit with a late fee at the local body — seek Registrar's permission.
  • After 1 year: Obtain an order from the First Class Magistrate or SDM of your district, then register at the local body.
  • Apply at: e-Nagar Palika Portal.

Discrepancies in names/spellings between Aadhaar and medical certificates, or difficulty obtaining affidavits for home deaths from the Gram Panchayat, lead to rejections.

How to fix:

  • Cross-check all documents — names and dates must be exactly consistent across MCCD, Aadhaar, and hospital records.
  • For home deaths, obtain an affidavit from the Gram Panchayat Pradhan or the local Patwari certifying the death.
  • If name discrepancy exists, prepare a Notarised Affidavit explaining the variation and submit it with your application.
  • Re-apply at e-Nagar Palika Portal.

Obtaining the certificate can take 10–20 days. Old records not found on the portal require a Non-Availability Certificate (NAC) before applying, extending the process.

How to fix:

  • Track your application at e-Nagar Palika Portal using your acknowledgement number.
  • For old records (pre-digitization), visit the Nagar Palika Records Room to request a manual search and obtain an NAC if not found.
  • With the NAC, apply for a delayed registration at the local body or through MP Online.
  • If processing exceeds 20 days, file a grievance via MP Samadhan Portal or call 181.

Reports indicate registrars or municipal staff may not follow fixed schedules. Corrections for errors (wrong date/spelling) also require a complex, slow process.

How to fix:

  • For unavailable officials, note the date and time of your visit and submit a written complaint to the District Collector's office.
  • Raise a grievance via MP Samadhan Portal against the specific office, citing dates of failed visits.
  • For certificate corrections: submit a Notarised Affidavit and supporting proof (hospital record, Aadhaar) to the Registrar's office.
  • If correction is refused without reason, escalate to the Chief Municipal Officer (CMO) of your city. Helpline: 181.
